persona.support_system: friends, but feels judged by them

information_stack.user_message: I feel like I’ve lost a part of myself. The weight’s piled on so fast that I can’t even recognize the mirror. Every time I see myself in the gym, I feel like I’m a stranger, and the comments from friends just make it worse. I’m not sure if I should keep going to the gym or just stay home.
information_stack.information: user feels unrecognizable due to rapid weight gain

information_stack.user_message: I keep looking at myself in the mirror and I feel like I’m staring at a stranger. The comments from friends feel like a harsh critique, and I can’t bring myself to go to the gym. I used to love running and feeling the wind, but now it feels like I’m betraying myself for not being able to keep up.
information_stack.information: user feels unrecognizable and disconnected from their body

information_stack.user_message: I’ve gained about 15 kilos in the last six months, and every time I look in the mirror I feel like I’m staring at a stranger. I’m scared that if I go to the gym people will notice and judge me, so I just stay home. It’s hard to find the motivation to exercise when I feel like I’m betraying the person I used to be.
information_stack.information: user feels unrecognizable and disconnected from their body
